era: 2005-present. DJ/electronic music programmer. E=5 -- kinetic electronic energy, dance-floor kinetics on independent radio. KEXP's electronic music voice. Capitol Hill.

location.formative: KEXP 90.3 FM. Capitol Hill clubs: Kremwerk, Timbre Room, Re-bar. Seattle's electronic underground. Warehouse parties and after-hours culture.
location.career: KEXP electronic music DJ (2005-present). Electronic music specialist. House, techno, ambient, IDM, UK bass, drum and bass, downtempo, experimental.

members.name: Indica Jones
members.note: KEXP's electronic music specialist. Deep subgenre knowledge: house, techno, ambient, IDM, UK bass, drum and bass, downtempo, experimental. Closes the KEXP radio sextet (130-135). Capitol Hill electronic culture.

associated_musicians.name: DJ B-Mello
associated_musicians.note: Street Sounds. Hip-hop/R&B. E=5. Same role as Indica Jones: genre specialist expanding KEXP beyond indie rock. B-Mello (hip-hop) and Indica Jones (electronic) are the two E=5 genre specialists in the KEXP sextet.

associated_musicians.name: Darek Mazzone
associated_musicians.note: Wo' Pop. World music. E=4. Three genre specialists: Mazzone (world), B-Mello (hip-hop), Indica Jones (electronic). All expand KEXP's genre coverage.

associated_musicians.name: Cheryl Waters
associated_musicians.note: The Midday Show. E=3. Opened the KEXP sextet. Indica Jones closes it. E=3 to E=5 -- the full energy arc.

associated_musicians.name: Kevin Cole
associated_musicians.note: KEXP Program Director. E=3. Designed the programming framework that creates space for genre specialists like Indica Jones.

associated_musicians.name: Nasty Nes Rodriguez
associated_musicians.note: KCMU hip-hop DJ. E=4. Opened the radio era (126). Indica Jones approaches its close (135). Ten degrees of radio infrastructure.

theory_nodes.concept: KEXP radio sextet CLOSES (130-135) -- six consecutive KEXP on-air personalities documenting the station's complete on-air infrastructure: Waters (130, Midday, local), Richards (131, Morning, personal), Mazzone (132, Wo' Pop, global), Cole (133, Program Director), B-Mello (134, hip-hop/R&B), Indica Jones (135, electronic); S36 transitions back to performing artists
examples: Host trio (130-132): Midday + Morning + Wo' Pop. Daily shows.
Institutional layer (133-135): Program Director + Hip-Hop DJ + Electronic DJ.
Energy arc: E=3, E=3, E=4, E=3, E=5, E=5. From warmth to kinetics.
Sextet complete. S36 transitions back to performing artists.
theory_nodes.description: KEXP radio sextet closes at degree 135. Six personalities, complete infrastructure.

ancestors: KEXP host trio (130-132). The first three of the sextet.
Radio era (126-135). Ten degrees of radio/DJ figures.
genealogy.relationship: Sextet closes at degree 135. Radio era approaches conclusion.

theory_nodes.concept: Electronic music curation on independent radio -- making the argument that radio can serve electronic music with depth and context; gateway to the underground; independent labels, DIY producers, subgenres beyond mainstream EDM; Capitol Hill's electronic culture as local anchor
examples: House, techno, ambient, IDM, UK bass, drum and bass, downtempo, experimental.
Most electronic discovery happens via streaming/clubs/festivals -- radio curation is rare.
Capitol Hill clubs (Kremwerk, Timbre Room, Re-bar) as local scene anchor.
Completing KEXP's genre spectrum: indie + world + hip-hop + electronic.
theory_nodes.description: Electronic music on independent radio. Deep subgenre curation. Capitol Hill connection.

ancestors: KEXP's curatorial ethos applied to electronic music.
genealogy.relationship: Electronic curation at degree 135. Fourth genre pillar of KEXP.

theory_nodes.concept: The DJ as energy architect -- electronic DJing as architectural craft: set construction, energy management, beatmatching, harmonic mixing, EQ transitions; fundamentally different from talk-and-play radio; the mix is the art; every transition is a design choice
examples: Building a set: managing energy levels, creating peaks and valleys.
Technical craft: beatmatching, harmonic mixing, EQ, effects.
Curatorial decisions as architectural decisions: key, BPM, texture, energy.
Continuous musical experience vs. song-by-song curation.
theory_nodes.description: DJ as energy architect. Set construction as performance art.

ancestors: DJ as translator (Mazzone, 132). Different DJ role, same craft lineage.
Electronic DJing vs. talk radio hosting. Fundamentally different skills.
genealogy.relationship: Energy architecture at degree 135. The performance dimension of DJing.

theory_nodes.concept: E=5 kinetic electronic energy -- dance-floor kinetics on radio; BPM-driven, bass-forward, build-peak-release; matches DJ B-Mello (134) at the genre-specialist level; higher than host trio (E=3/E=4); electronic music demands kinetic energy; still KEXP-calibrated, not festival-scale
examples: BPM spectrum: downtempo (70-100), house (120-130), techno (130-150), DnB (170-180).
E=5 matches B-Mello (134) -- both genre-specialist DJs at kinetic energy level.
Higher than Waters (130, E=3), Richards (131, E=3), Mazzone (132, E=4), Cole (133, E=3).
E=5 not E=6: still radio, not festival. KEXP-calibrated kinetic energy.
theory_nodes.description: E=5 kinetic electronic energy. Dance-floor kinetics on independent radio.

ancestors: E=4 adventurous DJ energy (Mazzone, 132). Electronic goes one higher.
E=5 at B-Mello (134). Same energy, different genre.
genealogy.relationship: E=5 electronic energy at degree 135. Kinetic but KEXP-calibrated.

cross_references.relationship: GENRE-SPECIALIST DJ PAIR! B-Mello (hip-hop/R&B, 134, E=5) and Indica Jones (electronic, 135, E=5). Same energy, same role: expanding KEXP beyond indie rock through DJ-driven programming.
GENRE EXPANSION LINEAGE! Mazzone (world, 132, E=4), B-Mello (hip-hop, 134, E=5), Indica Jones (electronic, 135, E=5). Three genre specialists completing KEXP's genre spectrum.
KEXP SEXTET BOOKENDS! Waters (130, E=3, Midday, local indie) opened the sextet. Indica Jones (135, E=5, electronic) closes it. E=3 to E=5 energy arc.
INSTITUTIONAL ARCHITECTURE! Cole (133, E=3, Program Director) designed the framework. Indica Jones (135, E=5, electronic) is one of the genre specialists that framework enables.
RADIO ERA LINEAGE! Nasty Nes (126, E=4) opened the radio era. Indica Jones (135, E=5) approaches its close. Ten degrees of radio infrastructure: 126-135.
